data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement check-precondition-emit.sh — a pre-commit hook using the DSO staged-diff scanning pattern that detects graceful-degradation trigger keywords in staged additions to the 11 target skill files and requires a nearby EMIT-PRECONDITIONS comment landmark.

**Why**: SC3 creates a developer-time enforcement layer so new graceful-degradation prose added to skill files always includes the required EMIT-PRECONDITIONS landmark, preventing the S2 instrumentation from going stale over time.

**Scope**:
- IN: Hook at plugins/dso/hooks/check-precondition-emit.sh following shape of plugins/dso/hooks/check-portability.sh; staged-diff scanning only (not whole-file — pre-existing prose not scrutinized); trigger keywords: "fall through", "safe fallback", "log a warning and continue", "continue without", "fall back", "graceful degradation"; check for EMIT-PRECONDITIONS landmark within N=10 lines in the same diff hunk; suppression annotation support (precondition-emit-ok comment on line bypasses per-line check); registration via 3aaa-0238 gate manifest if that epic ships first, else direct pre-commit wire with migration path documented; test fixtures with simulated git diff output
- OUT: Ack mechanism (S5); sprint closure check (S3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, adding any trigger keyword in a staged addition to a target file without an EMIT-PRECONDITIONS landmark within 10 lines in the same diff hunk fails the pre-commit hook with an instructive message
  ← Satisfies: "Detection rule: when a staged diff...ADDS lines containing any trigger keyword, the hook checks for an EMIT-PRECONDITIONS comment landmark within N=10 lines" (SC3)
- When this story is complete, a suppression annotation (precondition-emit-ok comment) on a trigger-keyword line bypasses the check for that specific line
  ← Satisfies: "Suppression annotation...bypasses the check on a per-line basis" (SC3)
- When this story is complete, pre-existing lines not in staged additions do not trigger the hook
  ← Satisfies: "The check scans STAGED DIFFS, not whole files — pre-existing prose is untouched" (SC3)
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Staged-diff behavior requires test fixtures with simulated git diff output (unified diff format); testing real git state in unit tests is fragile
- [Reliability] Boundary with 3aaa-0238 (CI gate manifest): document the integration path explicitly so either ordering (S4 before or after 3aaa-0238) is handled cleanly
- [Reliability] S4 MUST land after S2 is merged; S2 instruments all fallthrough sites with EMIT-PRECONDITIONS landmarks. If S4 lands before S2, the keyword detector fires on existing fallthrough prose (already present in the 11 files) and blocks all commits to those files (adversarial review finding)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a DSO plugin developer, the pre-commit hook check-precondition-emit.sh detects new graceful-degradation prose lacking an EMIT-PRECONDITIONS landmark so instrumentation debt cannot accumulate silently
